title: 25. Discord Voice Bridge
description: Set up the Discord Voice Bridge addon for either a real Discord voice channel or the bundled TinyMVP local test room.

steps.title: Open Discord Voice
steps.body: Discord Voice Bridge lets one or more NC bot personas listen and speak in a Discord voice channel, or in the local TinyMVP test room. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step in this tutorial panel if you want spoken guidance while you work.

steps.title: Enable the Addon
steps.body: Enable Discord Voice Bridge only when you want NC to launch bridge processes. Start on NC launch is optional; leave it off until your settings are known-good.

steps.title: Choose Real Discord or TinyMVP
steps.body: Bridge mode decides where the bots connect. Use HTTP / Discord for a real Discord voice channel. Use TinyMVP local room for local testing without Discord accounts, tokens, or a live server.

steps.title: Install Node Dependencies
steps.body: Real Discord mode needs the bundled Node bridge dependencies. If NC warns that dependencies are missing, accept the install prompt or use Install / Update Node Deps from the Status tab. TinyMVP mode does not need a Discord token.

steps.title: Enter Discord Connection Details
steps.body: For real Discord, create a Discord bot, invite it to your server, then enter the token source, guild ID, and voice channel ID here. Keep tokens local and do not commit settings.local.json.

steps.title: Use Lean Capture Settings
steps.body: For speech recognition, 16000 Hz mono WAV capture is usually enough and keeps files smaller. Keep low-information filtering enabled so short noise clips do not steer the room.

steps.title: Protect Turn Taking
steps.body: Playback and interruption settings decide when human speech interrupts a bot and when protected speech is only recorded into context. For a first test, keep coordinated bot replies enabled and avoid changing advanced timing while a bot is speaking.

steps.title: Configure Routing
steps.body: Shared Room Router decides who should answer next. LLM router mode is the normal multi-bot path. Group and open-room invitations let the conversation continue when someone asks the room instead of one named bot.

steps.title: Create Bot Personas
steps.body: Use the Bots tab to create one row per bot persona. Each bot can have its own Discord token, voice clone WAV, system prompt, and optional chat model. Start with one bot, then add more after the first bridge works.

steps.title: Try TinyMVP Locally
steps.body: TinyMVP local room is the safest first test for routing and moderation. Enable Open TinyMVP monitor window if you want a passive local room view when Start Bridge launches TinyMVP.

steps.title: Save Before Starting
steps.body: Save Settings writes the ignored local settings file used by the bridge launcher. Save before pressing Start Bridge, especially after editing bots, tokens, mode, or TinyMVP settings.

steps.title: Start and Watch Status
steps.body: Press Start Bridge and watch Status. You should see bot rows become running and connected. If something fails, Copy Diagnostics gives a redacted report that is safer to share than raw local settings.

steps.title: Use the Moderator Panel Later
steps.body: After normal routing works, use Moderator controls to set current and next speakers, allow only one speaker, route protected mic speech, or enforce Discord mute with a selected moderator bot. Leave hard moderation off until basic routing is stable.
